multi_step: Solving a midpoint geometry problem typically requires identifying the relevant triangle/segments in the figure and applying one or more midpoint relationships.
The question references three midpoint conditions (`J`, `P`, `L`), suggesting at least a small chain of geometric inferences rather than direct lookup.
No multiple-choice options are provided, so the answer likely comes from deriving a numeric relation from the diagram rather than selecting by elimination.
Because the image itself is not inspected here, the score is kept moderate rather than very high.

Geometry midpoint problems usually have rule-checkable solution logic if the diagram labels are available.
